steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure on each side of the blank
steps.content: Before the blank is “With over three millennia of climate data in its tree”—an introductory prepositional modifier that cannot stand alone. After the blank comes “a single tree like this,” the subject of the main clause, which must begin with an article.

steps.title: Apply the comma rule for introductory modifiers
steps.content:
Introductory prepositional modifiers must be separated from the main clause by a comma, and the subject must begin with an article.

The punctuation rings, a correctly provides the comma and the article “a” to complete this structure.

The other choices either end the introductory phrase with a period or semicolon, creating a fragment or breaking the rule that semicolons join only independent clauses, or incorrectly coordinate the introductory modifier with the main clause.

That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
